steps.title: Identify the sequential pattern
steps.content: The passage uses "first" to mark the initial step (scraping ice) and "Lastly" to mark the final step (smoothing). The blank introduces the middle step, so the transition must signal the second action in an ordered sequence.

steps.title: Test each choice against the pattern
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: signals that the sentence illustrates something from before, but the water spraying is not an example of scraping — it is a separate step. ✗
  • Choice B — Next,: signals the second step in a sequence, fitting exactly between "first" scraped and "Lastly" smoothed. ✓
  • Choice C — Similarly,: signals that this action resembles the previous one, but spraying water into grooves is not similar to scraping off ice. ✗
  • Choice D — In contrast,: signals an opposition to the previous action, but the passage describes steps in a single continuous process, not a contrast. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
